seo.title: Utah Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Utah — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license utah, dental assistant certification utah, RDA utah, DANB CDA utah, dental assistant radiography utah

board.name: Utah Division of Professional Licensing
board.mailing_address: Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing, P.O. Box 146741, Salt Lake City, UT 84114-6741

license_types.title: Licensed Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Utah.

steps.title: Understand Delegated Duties
steps.description: A dental assistant in Utah performs duties as delegated by a supervising licensed dentist, adhering to the scope outlined in the Utah Administrative Code Rule R156-69. There is no state application portal for general dental assistant licensing through the Utah DOPL.

steps.title: Meet Dentist's Requirements
steps.description: The hiring dentist is responsible for ensuring the dental assistant has the necessary skills, training, and competency for the tasks delegated, as no state-mandated license is required for the general dental assistant role.

steps.title: Consider Expanded Functions (If applicable)
steps.description: For specific expanded functions (e.g., coronal polishing, fluoride application, monitoring nitrous oxide), additional training, education, or certification may be required as specified by the supervising dentist or relevant regulations. However, these are generally not state licenses issued for the dental assistant profession itself.

steps.title: Maintain Professional Standards
steps.description: Adhere to the professional and ethical standards established for the dental profession in Utah, as outlined by the Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL) for all dental professionals.

steps.title: Employment with a Licensed Dentist
steps.description: Work under the direct or indirect supervision of a dentist who is actively licensed by the Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL). You can verify a dentist's license using the DOPL License Lookup system.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Licensed Dental Assistant through the Utah Division of Professional Licensing by license.

faqs.question: Does Utah require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Utah requires Licensed Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Utah?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Utah.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Utah?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Utah?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $55. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Licensed Dental Assistant through the Utah Division of Professional Licensing by license.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Utah
hero.dek: In Utah, general dental assistants do not require a state license or registration from the Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL). Their duties are delegated by a licensed dentist according to the Utah Dentist and Dental Hygienist Practice Act Rule.
